texto: These guys had names for every conceivable drinking situation. They liked to have a little eye-opener to get themselves going in the morning, a midmorning bracer before attempting anything serious, a few modest cocktails at lunch, followed by the obligatory afternoon pick-me-up, which segued neatly right into happy hour and ended with a little one just to help them sleep. For purely medicinal purposes, of course.
explicacao: Citação de G.M. Ford sobre temas ligados a felicidade. “These guys had names for every conceivable drinking situation. They liked to have a little eye-op…” articula felicidade, addiction, alcohol em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como bom-dia e termino.

texto: Buddy eyed me closely. His eyes were filigreed with red. I watched as he went through one of those instantaneous mood swings that only drunks and menstruating women can manage.

texto: Where’s Nadine?” “She went out for a walk the other day and didn’t come back.” “Really.” I tried to sound surprised. “No sweat,” he said. “Pussy may well be the only true renewable resource, Leo. I’ve got another one lined up for when I get back.” I had to admire a man with that kind of insight and planning.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de G.M. Ford que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Where’s Nadine?” “She went out for a walk the other day and didn’t come back.” “Really.” I tried …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, resourcefulness, sex, com foco em sex e women.

frase_original: Where’s Nadine?” “She went out for a walk the other day and didn’t come back.” “Really.” I tried to sound surprised. “No sweat,” he said. “Pussy may well be the only true renewable resource, Leo. I’ve got another one lined up for when I get back.” I had to admire a man with that kind of insight and planning.
